title: Fitting a Saniflo macerator pump to a WC
subtitle: Install a macerator unit to allow a WC to pump waste upward or over distance to the soil stack
excerpt: How to install a Saniflo or similar macerator pump to a WC in a location without a direct gravity connection to the soil stack. Covers choosing the correct unit, the waste connections, the power supply, and the maintenance access requirements.
sourceNotes: Saniflo installation and user guides (publicly available on manufacturer website). Building Regulations Approved Document H: Drainage and waste disposal. BS EN 12050-1: Wastewater lifting plants for buildings and sites.

glossaryTerms.definition: An electrical unit fitted behind or beside a WC pan that grinds waste from the WC (and optionally from a basin, shower, or bath) into a fine slurry and pumps it under pressure through a small-bore (22 or 32 mm) pipe to the nearest soil stack or sewer. Allows a WC to be installed in a location with no direct gravity drain connection — a basement, a cellar conversion, or a remote room.

The small-bore pipe from the macerator pump outlet to the soil stack connection. Standard diameter is 22 mm for shorter runs (up to 50 m horizontal or 4 m vertical) and 32 mm for longer runs. Must be fixed runs with no sags or traps — a trap in a macerator discharge pipe will fill with waste and block.

content.text: lets you add a WC almost anywhere in a building (cellar conversions, loft rooms, garages) without breaking up floors for a new drain run. The pump grinds and discharges waste through a 22 or 32 mm pipe to the nearest soil stack connection. Saniflo is the dominant UK brand; other manufacturers include Saniplus and WPump. The unit connects to a standard close-coupled WC pan and requires a switched, fused electrical supply.

A macerator unit rated for WC-only (Saniflo 001) or WC plus basin and shower (Saniflo Saniaccess range; choose by the number of inlet connections required). A close-coupled WC pan and cistern with a 90-degree horizontal pan outlet. Push-fit or compression fittings for the discharge pipe.
in MDPE or ABS push-fit. A fused spur or switched fused connection unit (3A fuse) for the macerator power supply. The cordless drill, spirit level, and tape measure.
Step 1, position the WC pan and macerator

Position the WC pan so its horizontal outlet connects directly to the macerator inlet; the pan should butt the macerator with no flexible connector in between. The macerator sits against the wall behind the WC. Mark the macerator fixing holes on the floor and wall and drill pilot holes. Check the pan is level on the floor (shim under the base if needed) and that the outlet aligns centrally with the macerator inlet.

Run the discharge pipe from the macerator outlet to the soil stack connection. The pipe must have a continuous rise or fall; never a sag or an inverted U, which creates a dead leg that blocks. If the pipe runs horizontally to a soil stack entry, it must rise at a minimum of 20 mm per metre to allow air into the pump discharge and prevent siphonage back into the unit. Fix the pipe in clips at 500 mm centres. At the soil stack, fit a boss adaptor entry above the lowest WC connection on the stack.

The macerator requires a 13A or 3A fused electrical connection, depending on the model. The supply must be switched; either by the macerator's own built-in pressure switch (standard) or via an external fused spur. Connect the unit to the supply per the manufacturer's wiring diagram. The unit should be on a circuit protected by an RCD.

Connect the cold water supply to the cistern. Restore the mains electrical supply. Flush the WC and listen: the macerator should fire after a brief delay (1 to 3 seconds), run for 5 to 15 seconds, and stop cleanly with no residual noise. Check all pipe connections for leaks. Do not flush anything other than WC waste and toilet paper; wet wipes, cotton wool, and sanitary products will destroy the macerator blade within months.

attrs.symptom: Macerator fails to start after flushing
attrs.cause: Pressure switch not activating, or electrical supply issue.
attrs.fix: Check the fused spur is on and the fuse is intact. If the supply is confirmed and the unit still does not start, the pressure switch inside the unit may need cleaning or replacing — isolate the unit and refer to the service manual.

attrs.symptom: WC waste backing up into the pan
attrs.cause: Discharge pipe is blocked or the macerator blade is jammed.
attrs.fix: Isolate the unit from the mains. Do not flush. Inspect the discharge pipe for blockages. If the pipe is clear, the macerator itself requires descaling or professional service.
